description: First rubric-based CV scoring batch — 4 anchor categories. Uses existing outputs with ambiguous provenance (accepted tradeoff). All 4 pairs use jobs_all[0] from each category as the JD exemplar. The scorer will catch category/persona mismatch via rubric-grounded penalties, which is the validation signal this batch is meant to produce.

pairs.selection_notes: No architect-first AI CV exists in outputs/. Using cMatter 'Head of Engineering — AI Platform' CV: closest available persona (platform/AI framing). Scorer should flag persona inflation (Head vs architect), weak AI architecture specifics, and title mismatch for this IC architect category. This is deliberate signal, not a clean fit.

cMatter 'Head of Engineering — AI Platform' CV has the closest persona match to head_of_ai_global (architect-first player-coach-safe per rubric). JD is a senior engineer ML role (category has weak title homogeneity, flagged upstream); scorer should still evaluate against the category rubric's player-coach framing.

5V 'Principal Engineer — Streaming Platform' CV is senior IC in persona (matches staff/principal rubric framing) but streaming-platform scope is weak fit for ML/search JD. Scorer should penalize JD alignment while crediting IC-authority executive_presence. Deliberate mismatch to validate anti-hallucination and JD-alignment scoring.

KAIZEN 'Software Engineering Team Lead — Backend' CV matches player-coach tech-lead persona. JD is Samsara ML tech-lead role; CV lacks explicit ML/AI signal, so scorer should flag JD-alignment gaps without penalizing persona framing.
